title: helianthemum croceum subsp. urrielense
intro: Helianthemum croceum subsp. urrielense is a subspecies of the rockrose plant, specifically native to the Iberian Peninsula, which is a shrub-like perennial with showy, pink to yellow flowers. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. Its health benefits also extend to soothing digestive issues, reducing fever, and aiding in wound healing. Helianthemum croceum subsp. urrielense can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures for its medicinal uses.
